title: Family Leave

jokes.text: While at a tech company famous for providing access to any information, except for its own name
jokes.setup: Tech company provides access to any information
jokes.punchline: Except its own name
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical jab at Google's secretive nature. Excellent economy and structure with a perfect ironic twist.

jokes.text: This class is geared more to self-defense for the family, not... Not from the family.
jokes.setup: Michael taking self-defense class
jokes.punchline: Instructor realizes it's from his family, not for them
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Michael's dysfunctional family dynamic. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: Whose vertigo made it hard for her to stay on her own feet
jokes.setup: Just till I get on my feet
jokes.punchline: Vertigo prevents her from staying on her feet
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay callback to 'get on my feet' but fairly straightforward pun structure.

jokes.text: At the worst possible time. That's not a great sign.
jokes.setup: Starting housing business during housing market collapse
jokes.punchline: Michael's massive understatement of the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic understated Michael response to disaster, but predictable structure.

jokes.text: George Mi... Harris
jokes.setup: George Michael needs to give a fake name to Rebel
jokes.punchline: He awkwardly combines his real name with a fake surname
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Michael's awkwardness and inability to fully lie. Sets up major plot point.

jokes.text: Maeby, we can't afford this. And my software isn't even rea... dy.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to fire Maeby while admitting financial troubles
jokes.punchline: Almost admits his software doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Michael's dishonesty catching up to him. The hesitation sells it.

jokes.text: She was raised by a different mother than Ron's other kids. It's just a whole different thing over there.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining Rebel's family situation
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of complex family dynamics as just 'different'
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to oversimplify complex situations, especially ironic given his own family chaos.

jokes.text: That one's hard to explain.
jokes.setup: Mention of hiding in a gorilla suit
jokes.punchline: Narrator acknowledging the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the show's own complexity. Classic AD self-awareness.

jokes.text: I'm gonna have sex with you, for money. That perhaps overestimated his value, at least according to the latest Balboa Bachelor Auction totals.
jokes.setup: Michael's desperate proposal
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals he overvalued himself based on auction prices
jokes.explanation: Dark, unexpected punchline that completely undermines Michael's proposal. Perfect narrator timing.

jokes.text: Some of the neighbors are not to be desired. Oh! One got in.
jokes.setup: Realtor showing property, complaining about undesirable neighbors
jokes.punchline: Sees unconscious Michael on floor
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag where drugged Michael becomes the exact problem the realtor was describing.

jokes.text: Boy, you guys don't miss a day. Michael had missed two.
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on pest control workers' dedication
jokes.punchline: He's the one who missed days, not them
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic reversal. Michael's obliviousness to his own behavior.

jokes.text: Are you okay? Yes, I'm okay. I've wanted to ask you that underwater.
jokes.setup: Teaching underwater communication
jokes.punchline: Michael's bizarre priority of asking this specific question underwater
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Michael logic - overthinking a simple father-son activity.

jokes.text: Ironically, it was this final threat that did young George Michael in.
jokes.setup: Teaching about electric eel danger
jokes.punchline: George Michael faints from the lesson, not actual danger
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist where the warning about danger becomes the danger.

jokes.text: George Michael? George Michael? Hey, pal. Here we go.
jokes.setup: Michael finds young George Michael apparently drowned in pool
jokes.punchline: It's another fake-out drowning (running gag from series)
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to series running gag. Michael's resigned reaction shows this is routine.

jokes.text: I thought it was a restaurant by the pool. Why did you think I said, 'Watch out for the crabs'? Why did you think I said, 'I always get a hamburger'?
jokes.setup: George Michael thought 'the Reef' was a restaurant
jokes.punchline: His increasingly absurd explanations for ocean warnings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating misunderstanding. Child logic perfectly applied to recontextualize ocean dangers as restaurant experiences.

jokes.text: Am I that much of a monster, Michael? Even to you? No, not at all, I just wasn't expecting, uh, you know, a man to be sitting in a Scuba Jr., leaping out at me up in the attic, that's all.
jokes.setup: Buster asking if he's a monster
jokes.punchline: Michael's overly specific, understated explanation of why he was startled
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael diplomatic response. The specificity makes the absurd situation sound reasonable.

jokes.text: No, I just took the skin off. It's in the dryer.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Buster got a new hand
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of removing and laundering fake skin
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly casual delivery of disturbing information. Perfect Buster character moment treating the bizarre as mundane.

jokes.text: No, pal, we were gonna use this scuba gear for scuba diving. Underwater. Not to hang out in a poison cloud.
jokes.setup: Buster suggesting scuba gear for fumigation protection
jokes.punchline: Michael having to explain scuba diving's actual purpose
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Michael's exasperation at having to explain obvious things to Buster.

jokes.text: We used to want to play dress-up. Her as Jackie O., me as John-John... But we... No, I guess we did get to do that a couple of times. ♪ Motherboy... ♪
jokes.setup: Buster talking about regrets with his mother
jokes.punchline: Realizes they did do the disturbing activity, with musical sting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment with callback to recurring creepy mother relationship. The musical sting enhances the discomfort.

jokes.text: Pick a lane, Michael! You can't say I can't be here and say you're not looking after me. Pick a lane, Michael!
jokes.setup: Michael's contradictory statements about Buster staying
jokes.punchline: Buster using 'pick a lane' repeatedly, illogically
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Buster's misuse of the phrase. Character-appropriate confusion.

jokes.text: Hope I never do that to my own, uh... um... Mom. Son. Ah. Oh, right. Hope I never do that to my son.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing his mother's behavior
jokes.punchline: Almost says he hopes he doesn't do it to his 'mom' instead of son
jokes.explanation: Subtle but brilliant moment showing Michael's psychology - his mother relationship so dominant he confuses it with his son.

jokes.text: Pick a lane, Michael. Pick a lane, Buster. No, you pick a lane! Come on. Ow, ow, ow. I picked a lane!
jokes.setup: The 'pick a lane' argument continues
jokes.punchline: It escalates into physical comedy with scuba gear
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the verbal argument into physical comedy with the cumbersome scuba gear.

jokes.text: Hey. What are you doing here? And almost immediately found himself helplessly doing the opposite. I'm dating Rebel Alley.
jokes.setup: Michael determined to be honest and end things
jokes.punchline: Immediately lies and makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Michael's inability to follow through on good intentions. Great narrator setup.

jokes.text: Like when you punch your dad.
jokes.setup: Narrator discussing moments that create family earthquakes
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions punching your dad as example
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of shocking family violence as casual example. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Like when you punch your dad.
jokes.setup: Narrator discusses moments that create seismic family shifts
jokes.punchline: Cut to George Michael punching Michael as example
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual punctuation to narrator's philosophical observation. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: Ciao. Bella.
jokes.setup: Father and son trying to end extremely awkward encounter
jokes.punchline: Split 'ciao bella' between them awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward ending to traumatic encounter. The split Italian phrase emphasizes their discomfort.

jokes.text: Boy. It's like the Weimar Republic in that place.
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on Lucille 2's apartment
jokes.punchline: Pretentious historical reference for apartment noise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael pretension - using historical reference for mundane observation. Shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: I'm doing a scrub-down. Nobody can know I was here, having a little smooch with my boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining why she can't talk
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of criminal evidence destruction and relationship
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Maeby's casual attitude toward illegal activity.

jokes.text: While she believed that he was an undercover cop posing as a teenager. A señor? I'm 17. Ha! He turned actually out... to be a teenager, posing as an undercover cop, posing as a teenager.
jokes.setup: Explaining Maeby's boyfriend's identity
jokes.punchline: Absurd triple layer of disguise/deception
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity. The layers of deception are perfectly convoluted in AD style.

jokes.text: Just panties, leggings, corsets, the sex knobs, and one of those double-headed bondage things with the curly choke leash. That's probably a landline.
jokes.setup: Maeby listing incriminating items to remove
jokes.punchline: Realizes the 'bondage thing' is just a phone
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection where sex toy description perfectly matches old phone. Visual gag works on rewatch.

jokes.text: You drive her car. How do you still not know who that is?
jokes.setup: Maeby confused about Lucille 2 identity
jokes.punchline: George Michael pointing out the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple logical observation highlighting Maeby's obliviousness. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You're burning bridges all over the place.
jokes.setup: George Michael punched his dad
jokes.punchline: Maeby's casual assessment of his relationship destruction
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment but fairly standard observation. Good economy.

jokes.text: Well, for starters, you could post it on Dadfights.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking what to do about punching his dad
jokes.punchline: Suggestion to post it on social media site for dad violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy creating fake social media site for family violence. Perfect absurd solution.

jokes.text: It's just, like, a bunch of guys punching their dads, or...? Yeah, well, you're supposed to say, 'Guess what, Dad,' but some dads were catching on, and they were just whaling on their kids. You can watch those clips on That'sWhatSon.
jokes.setup: Explaining Dadfights website
jokes.punchline: Counter-site for dads hitting kids, with perfect name
jokes.explanation: Exceptional escalation creating perfect counter-website. The name 'That'sWhatSon' is brilliant wordplay on 'that's what she said.'

jokes.text: Forget, but never forgive. You think she meant to say it that way?
jokes.setup: Maeby quoting Lucille's advice
jokes.punchline: Questioning if the backwards saying was intentional
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Lucille's character trait of mangling common sayings. The questioning adds meta-humor.

jokes.text: They should take the rapists and murderers and put them all together on an island... and the murderers can be raped, the rapists can be murdered... until you only have either two rapists or one murderer. One raped murderer but who cares about him?
jokes.setup: More of Lucille's backwards wisdom
jokes.punchline: Escalating dark logic about criminal island
jokes.explanation: Dark escalating logic that's both disturbing and absurd. Perfect Lucille character callback with mathematical precision to violence.

jokes.text: Isn't today Cinco? Today's the sixth.
jokes.setup: Officer mentions Lucille 2 missing since Cinco
jokes.punchline: Michael's confusion about what day it is (due to being drugged)
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Michael's continued confusion from being drugged. Advances plot.

jokes.text: But they just don't pay fair. I do okay. Hmm? Yeah, I'm sure you do. But, you know, you probably... you haven't had to pay for a meal in years, though. Huh?
jokes.setup: Michael saying he wanted to be a cop
jokes.punchline: Awkward assumption about free meals corruption
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's inappropriate assumptions about police corruption. Cringe-inducing in character.

jokes.text: It's the way that I say goodbye. Um... because you never know, um... if you're never gonna see someone again. You should know that. You see people actually die. I've seen one person die. A postman.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he wrote 'Love you' on note
jokes.punchline: Elaborately justifying it, then cop's mundane death experience
jokes.explanation: Good escalating discomfort as Michael digs deeper. The postman detail deflates his dramatic justification.

jokes.text: I don't allow men in my apartment without my husband present.
jokes.setup: George Sr. disguised as woman to hide low testosterone
jokes.punchline: Attempting feminine voice and mannerisms
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag concept but fairly standard cross-dressing comedy. Character-appropriate for George Sr.'s cowardice.

jokes.text: What do you call it? Um... What do you call it? Brains. Long-term brains.
jokes.setup: Gob forgetting word for memory storage
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'long-term brains'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob stupidity moment. The redundant phrase 'long-term brains' is perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Here? I'm just getting some boner pills... and this guys says I don't need them. He knows from boner...
jokes.setup: George Sr. asks what Gob is doing in Mexico
jokes.punchline: Gob's characteristic oversharing about erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob oversharing with his father. Perfect character consistency in inappropriate context.

jokes.text: Mexico won't be able to walk for a week. Two full weeks!
jokes.setup: Gob suggests they 'work their way through Mexico'
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s disturbing sexual boast, then escalation
jokes.explanation: Disturbing but funny escalation between father and son. Shows their competitive dysfunction.

jokes.text: Mexico won't be able to walk for a week. Two full weeks!
jokes.setup: Gob suggesting they work their way through Mexico
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s misunderstanding about sexual conquest of country
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with George Sr. misinterpreting travel as sexual conquest. Escalation with 'two weeks' adds to absurdity.

jokes.text: Of course, I'm holding a wig that is made of red hair. So that's those two and the wig, I think that's got it. And my sister has recently gotten the red hair. But she's nuts, so...
jokes.setup: Michael listing people he knows with red hair
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions holding red wig while listing, dismisses sister as nuts
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list with Michael's obliviousness to evidence in his hands. Casual dismissal of Lindsay is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Of course, I'm holding a wig that is made of red hair. So that's those two and the wig, I think that's got it.
jokes.setup: Officer asks about people with red hair
jokes.punchline: Michael literally holds red wig while listing red-haired people
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with Michael's oblivious honesty making him look more suspicious.

jokes.text: It's A-N-U-S and then for some reason T-A-R-T. 'A new start.' If you saw it written out, I don't think that you would...
jokes.setup: Cop asking about license plate
jokes.punchline: Michael spells out ANUSTART, explaining the intended meaning
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to iconic visual gag. Michael's obliviousness to the obvious reading makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Let me try this one on you. A-N-A-L-R-A-P...
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of inappropriate license plates
jokes.punchline: Cop starts spelling even worse plate combination
jokes.explanation: Escalation of license plate gag but less clever than original. Still functional comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, they have one.
jokes.setup: Lindsay arriving at border after calling for wall
jokes.punchline: Surprised discovery that wall already exists
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment showing Lindsay's political ignorance. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: How do you say 'I'm American' in Mexican? Soy americano. Hmm. That does sound good. Ask him for two.
jokes.setup: Lindsay needs to say she's American in Spanish
jokes.punchline: Thinks 'soy americano' is a food order
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Lindsay's perfect combination of ignorance and entitlement. The misunderstanding is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Soy americano. Hmm. That does sound good. Ask him for two.
jokes.setup: Driver translates 'I'm American' as 'Soy americano'
jokes.punchline: Lindsay thinks it's a food order and wants two
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment showing her complete cultural cluelessness. Excellent wordplay with 'soy'.

jokes.text: That little bald one there with the fringed cut-offs who... Yay! Oh,. It's you.
jokes.setup: Lucille complaining about annoying person on beach
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's describing Tobias, who appears
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff with Lucille unknowingly describing her companion. Visual element enhances the joke.

jokes.text: That little bald one there with the fringed cut-offs who... Yay! Oh. It's you.
jokes.setup: Lucille ranting about annoying beach person to therapist
jokes.punchline: She realizes she's describing Tobias, who's right there
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and payoff with perfect character moment. Lucille's oblivious cruelty revealed perfectly.

tmdb_overview: Surprises greet Michael at the penthouse and Sudden Valley. George-Michael confides in Maeby, who's trying to avoid Sudden Valley.
insight: Season 5 restarts with 83-point character comedy: nearly 90 jokes across 53 minutes.
